message:
audit: fcp-core/error structurally safe; one MEDIUM noted for boundary-scrub design

Reviewed FcpError, ErrorCategory, FcpErrorResponse, and the to_response /
details / Display / Serde surface.

Display safety: thiserror-generated Display only interpolates named fields
with structured placeholders; no Debug-format escapes, no field-eliding
catchalls. No variant carries an inherently sensitive type — all secret-
adjacent surface is a caller-supplied String.

Coercion paths (sampled across drive/gmail/calendar/github error modules)
preserve message: String from the source error verbatim; no extra context
amplifies a leak.

[MEDIUM] structural risk noted (no patch shipped): External::message and
the other String-bearing variants propagate caller-supplied content
verbatim through both Display→logs and to_response→wire (line 571).
Most-exposed path is External, typically built from
underlying_http_error.to_string(); if the underlying client's Display
includes URLs with ?access_token=… query params or Authorization-header
values, those secrets reach the caller via FcpErrorResponse. Mitigation
requires per-call-site discipline OR a boundary scrubber; both are
design decisions outside the scope of a review patch. Worth a future
bead if leak telemetry surfaces a concrete instance.

[LOW] notes (cosmetic, not shipped):
- CapabilityDenied::reason duplicated on the wire (in both details JSON
  and the to_string-derived message).
- FcpError derives Serialize/Deserialize itself, so direct JSON
  serialization of an FcpError exposes more than the FcpErrorResponse
  wire spec intends.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
